title: Use Short-Circuit Logic in Boolean Contexts
description: Utilizing non-short-circuit logic in a boolean context is likely a mistake that could lead to serious program errors. This occurs when conditions are evaluated under incorrect circumstances. Always ensure to use short-circuit logic in boolean contexts to avoid this issue.
recommendation: Replace non-short-circuit logic operators with short-circuit ones. For instance, replace '&' with '&&' and '|' with '||' where appropriate.
impact: Using non-short-circuit logic can lead to unexpected behavior in code execution, potentially causing serious program errors. It can affect the reliability and robustness of the software.

checks.message: Replace non-short-circuit logic operators: use '&&' instead of '&' and '||' instead of '|' in boolean contexts
